(Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- docs-consistency-guard: Documentation agent that audits docs for staleness and inconsistency AND generates/updates documentation on request. Use proactively when reviewing any PR that touches CLAUDE.md, docs/adr/, CONTRIBUTING.md, or docs/architecture.md. Also invoke when the user asks "is the documentation up to date?", "update the milestone", "scaffold the ADR for X", "add this to the ADR index", "add a TODO item", "scaffold a README for X", or "create a doc stub for Y." (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash, Write, Edit)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- migration-safety: Specialized reviewer for Alembic migrations under backend/alembic/versions/. Audits for backward-incompatible operations that would break running code during a deploy, per working-agreement (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash)

This agent should be used proactively after writing or modifying code, especially before committing changes or creating pull requests. It will check for style violations, potential issues, and ensure code follows the established patterns in CLAUDE.md. Also the agent needs to know which files to focus on for the review. In most cases this will be recently completed work which is unstaged in git (can be retrieved by running git diff). Typical triggers include the user asking for a review of a feature they just implemented, the assistant proactively reviewing its own newly-written code before declaring a task done, and a final pre-PR check before opening a pull request. ## claude.ai Microsoft Learn
# Microsoft Learn MCP Server

This server gives structured access to official Microsoft and Azure documentation via three tools:

## Tools

### microsoft_docs_search
Search official documentation and return up to 10 concise, high-quality content chunks (max 500 tokens each), including title, URL, and excerpt.

- Use first to get a quick, reliable overview
- Ideal for grounding answers in Microsoft knowledge

### microsoft_code_sample_search
Search for code snippets and examples in official Microsoft Learn documentation and return up to 20 relevant, high-quality code samples.

- Use when you need to provide sample Microsoft/Azure related code in your answers.  
- Ideal for generating code snippets or practical implementation examples.  
- Optional parameter `language` can filter results. 

### microsoft_docs_fetch
Fetch and convert full Microsoft documentation pages to markdown.

- Use after search when you need full content from a specific URL
- Required for detailed tutorials, troubleshooting, prerequisites, code samples, or when search results are incomplete or outdated

## Workflow

1. Use `microsoft_docs_search` to find relevant documents.
2. If you need code examples or practical snippets, use `microsoft_code_sample_search`.
3. If deeper or complete information is needed, use `microsoft_docs_fetch`.

**Search gives breadth. Code Sample Search gives practical examples. Fetch gives depth.**

All content comes from Microsoft Learn or official sources, returned in clean markdown format.

- commit-commands:commit-push-pr: Commit, push, and open a PR
- commit-commands:commit: Create a git commit
- code-review:code-review: Code review a pull request
- pr-review-toolkit:review-pr: Comprehensive PR review using specialized agents
- claude-code-setup:claude-automation-recommender: Analyze a codebase and recommend Claude Code automations (hooks, subagents, skills, plugins, MCP servers). Use when user asks for automation recommendations, wants to optimize their Claude Code setup, mentions improving Claude Code workflows, asks how to first set up Claude Code for a project, or wants to know what Claude Code features they should use.
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Guidance for distinctive, intentional visual design when building new UI or reshaping an existing one. Helps with aesthetic direction, typography, and making choices that don't read as templated defaults.
- skill-creator:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

=== deploy.yml ===
name: Deploy

# Manual-only for now (workflow_dispatch): apply-ready scaffolding that never
# fires on its own until the Azure resources exist + secrets/vars are set. Once
# the infra is provisioned (see deploy/README.md), flip on the push trigger:
#   push:
#     branches: [main]
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      image_tag:
        description: "Image tag to build + deploy"
        default: "latest"

concurrency:
  group: deploy-production
  cancel-in-progress: false

# OIDC federated login to Azure (no stored client secret). The federated cred +
# the listed vars/secrets must be configured first — see deploy/README.md.
# packages:write lets the GITHUB_TOKEN push the backend image to GHCR (ADR 0023).
permissions:
  id-token: write
  contents: read
  packages: write

env:
  # GHCR registry (ADR 0023) — public package, lowercase owner. ACA pulls it
  # anonymously, so no registry credential is stored on the apps/job.
  IMAGE: ghcr.io/theurgicduke771/dataq-backend:${{ inputs.image_tag }}

jobs:
  backend:
    name: Backend — build, migrate, deploy (API + worker)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: production
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Azure login (OIDC)
        uses: azure/login@v2
        with:
          client-id: ${{ secrets.AZURE_CLIENT_ID }}
          tenant-id: ${{ secrets.AZURE_TENANT_ID }}
          subscription-id: ${{ secrets.AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID }}

      # Push to GHCR (ADR 0023). The GITHUB_TOKEN has packages:write on this repo's
      # registry; no Azure/ACR credential is involved in the image push.
      - name: Log in to GHCR
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ghcr.io
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Build + push backend image to GHCR
        run: |
          docker build -f backend/Dockerfile -t "$IMAGE" .
          docker push "$IMAGE"

      # Migrate BEFORE rolling the app: migrations are additive/backward-compatible
      # (CLAUDE.md), so the running old code tolerates the new schema. Runs the
      # one-off migrate Container Apps Job (provisioned per deploy/README.md).
      # `az containerapp job start` only *starts* the execution and returns, so we
      # capture the execution name and poll it to a terminal state — the rollout
      # below must not begin until the migration has actually SUCCEEDED, and a
      # failed migration must abort the deploy (fail-closed) rather than roll the
      # apps onto an un-migrated schema.
      - name: Run DB migrations (alembic upgrade head) and wait for success
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          exec_name=$(az containerapp job start \
            --name "${{ vars.MIGRATE_JOB_NAME }}" \
            --resource-group "${{ vars.AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP }}" \
            --image "$IMAGE" \
            --query name -o tsv)
          echo "Started migration execution: $exec_name"
          deadline=$(( SECONDS + 900 ))  # 15-minute cap
          while (( SECONDS < deadline )); do
            status=$(az containerapp job execution list \
              --name "${{ vars.MIGRATE_JOB_NAME }}" \
              --resource-group "${{ vars.AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP }}" \
              --query "[?name=='$exec_name'].properties.status | [0]" -o tsv)
            echo "migration status: ${status:-<pending>}"
            case "$status" in
              Succeeded) echo "Migration succeeded."; exit 0 ;;
              Failed|Degraded|Stopped) echo "::error::Migration $status — aborting before app rollout."; exit 1 ;;
            esac
            sleep 10
          done
          echo "::error::Timed out waiting for migration to complete."; exit 1

      - name: Deploy API
        run: |
          az containerapp update \
            --name "${{ vars.API_APP_NAME }}" \
            --resource-group "${{ vars.AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP }}" \
            --image "$IMAGE"

      - name: Deploy worker
        run: |
          az containerapp update \
            --name "${{ vars.WORKER_APP_NAME }}" \
            --resource-group "${{ vars.AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP }}" \
            --image "$IMAGE"

  frontend:
    name: Frontend — build + deploy to Static Web App
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: production
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "22"
          cache: pnpm
          cache-dependency-path: frontend/pnpm-lock.yaml

      - name: Build
        working-directory: frontend
        # VITE_* are inlined at build time. Non-secret azure client/tenant ids
        # come from repo vars; never the SPA secret (there is none — MSAL is public).
        env:
          VITE_AZURE_TENANT_ID: ${{ vars.VITE_AZURE_TENANT_ID }}
          VITE_AZURE_SPA_CLIENT_ID: ${{ vars.VITE_AZURE_SPA_CLIENT_ID }}
          VITE_AZURE_API_CLIENT_ID: ${{ vars.VITE_AZURE_API_CLIENT_ID }}
          VITE_AZURE_API_SCOPE: ${{ vars.VITE_AZURE_API_SCOPE }}
          VITE_AUTH_DEV_BYPASS: "false"
        run: |
          pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
          pnpm build

      - name: Deploy to Azure Static Web Apps
        uses: Azure/static-web-apps-deploy@v1
        with:
          azure_static_web_apps_api_token: ${{ secrets.AZURE_STATIC_WEB_APPS_API_TOKEN }}
          action: upload
          app_location: frontend/dist
          skip_app_build: true

    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

        uses: Azure/static-web-apps-deploy@v1
        with:

file.content:
# DataQ backend image — serves both the FastAPI API and the Celery worker.
# Build context is the repo ROOT (not backend/) because the app imports as the
# top-level `backend.app.*` package:
#   docker build -f backend/Dockerfile .
#
# Multi-stage pip build on python:3.13-slim (ADR 0025). The production image uses
# pip, NOT conda: every dependency already came through pip (environment.yml only
# `-r`'d the requirements chain — conda installed no conda-channel packages), so
# the miniconda base added ~1.5-2GB and build friction for no benefit. Conda stays
# the LOCAL-DEV tool (environment.yml / setup.sh) per CLAUDE.md; only this image
# changed. RUNTIME deps only (requirements.txt) — tests/tooling run in the dev env
# (conda locally, pip in CI), never in this image.

# ── Builder: install deps into an isolated venv ──────────────────────────────
FROM python:3.13-slim AS builder

ENV PIP_NO_CACHE_DIR=1 \
    PIP_DISABLE_PIP_VERSION_CHECK=1

# build-essential covers any sdist-only transitive dep; it lives ONLY in this
# stage and never reaches the final image.
RUN apt-get update \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends build-essential \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

RUN python -m venv /opt/venv
ENV PATH=/opt/venv/bin:$PATH

# Copy just the requirements chain first so this layer caches on pin changes
# only, not on every code edit. requirements.txt is the runtime SINGLE SOURCE OF
# TRUTH (the dev/typecheck/tooling files are not installed into the image).
COPY backend/requirements*.txt ./backend/
RUN pip install -r backend/requirements.txt

# ── Runtime: slim base + the venv + app code ─────────────────────────────────
FROM python:3.13-slim AS runtime

# Link the GHCR package to this repo (shows under the repo's Packages and lets the
# repo's Actions GITHUB_TOKEN push to it — ADR 0023/0024). Without this the package
# is user-scoped + unlinked, so CI can't push and ACA-pull access isn't inherited.
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.source="https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.description="DataQ backend (FastAPI API + Celery worker)"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.licenses="MIT"

ENV PATH=/opt/venv/bin:$PATH \
    PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
    PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1

WORKDIR /workspace

# The fully-built venv from the builder (no compilers / apt lists in this layer).
COPY --from=builder /opt/venv /opt/venv

# Application code + tooling config (pyproject is imported for runtime config).
COPY pyproject.toml ./
COPY backend ./backend

EXPOSE 8000

# Default to the API; the worker overrides `command` (compose / Container App),
# and the migrate job runs `alembic upgrade head`. psycopg2-binary (a wheel)
# keeps the image apt-free; the psycopg2-source swap remains a deferred nicety.
CMD ["uvicorn", "backend.app.main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000"]

Diagnosed it. Here's the situation:

**State now**
- Package `ghcr.io/theurgicduke771/dataq-backend` exists, **public**, 15 versions (tags `v1`–`v4`), but the API shows **no `repository` field** → genuinely unlinked.
- The `org.opencontainers.image.source` label *is* correctly set in the final `runtime` stage of [backend/Dockerfile:41](backend/Dockerfile#L41), and it landed in commit `eec1105`.

**Why the label didn't auto-link**
The 15 versions weren't pushed by this repo's Actions — they were pushed manually (local `docker push` during the Terraform deploy). GHCR's label-based auto-link is unreliable, and more importantly the source label lives in the **image config blob**, not as a manifest-level annotation, so it didn't trigger a connect on those pushes.

**The blocker can't be fixed from CLI.** There is no GitHub REST/GraphQL endpoint to (a) connect a package to a repo or (b) grant a repo's `GITHUB_TOKEN` push access. Both are UI-only. This is exactly the "one-time GHCR package→repo connect" item still open in CLAUDE.md.

**What you need to do (≈30 seconds, browser):**

Go to → https://github.com/users/TheurgicDuke771/packages/container/dataq-backend/settings

1. **Connect Repository** → pick `TheurgicDuke771/DataQ`. (Makes it show under the repo + inherit visibility — the "link" you're asking about.)
2. **Manage Actions access** → Add Repository → `DataQ` → role **Write**. *This* is what actually lets the `deploy.yml` `GITHUB_TOKEN` (`packages: write`) push. Connecting alone doesn't grant Actions push.

Do **both** — #1 is the visible link, #2 is what unblocks CI.

---

Optional durable hardening I *can* do in-repo: change the deploy build step from raw `docker build`/`docker push` to `docker/build-push-action` so it emits `org.opencontainers.image.source` as a **manifest annotation** (the reliable auto-link signal) on every future push. It won't retroactively link the existing versions or replace the manual Actions-access grant, but it prevents re-drift. Want me to make that change?

content.text:
The GHCR test is the part you asked about and it **passed**. But letting the deploy finish surfaced **two pre-existing deploy bugs**, both unrelated to GHCR — this was the first true end-to-end run of `deploy.yml` (the real deploy was done via local Terraform before), so they'd never executed until now:

**1. Backend — `alembic upgrade head` migrate job Failed** (rollout correctly aborted, fail-closed). Need the job's container logs for the cause. Let me try to pull them.

**2. Frontend — `pnpm/action-setup@v4` errored: "No pnpm version is specified."** The action needs either `with: version:` or a `packageManager` field in `package.json`, and neither is present. Pure workflow/config bug.

Let me get the migration failure reason.

Found it — and the migration "failure" is misleading. The migrate job **didn't run Alembic at all**; it booted the image's default `uvicorn` CMD, which then died on `RuntimeError: Auth not configured`. So the real defects are:

Let me confirm the two root causes in the source.

content.text:
Both root causes confirmed. Neither is a GHCR or image problem — the image is fine. They're two **`deploy.yml` workflow bugs**, and this run was the first time the workflow actually executed those steps:

**Bug 1 — migrate job runs the wrong command.**
Terraform defines the job correctly — [containerapps.tf:187](deploy/terraform/containerapps.tf#L187) sets `command = ["sh","-c","cd backend && alembic upgrade head"]`. But the workflow starts it with `az containerapp job start --name ... --image "$IMAGE"` ([deploy.yml](.github/workflows/deploy.yml)). Passing `--image` to `job start` replaces the container template **without** the configured command, so it falls back to the image's default Docker CMD (`uvicorn`) — which boots the API, hits `init_auth()`, and dies with `Auth not configured`. Alembic never ran.
Fix: `az containerapp job update --image "$IMAGE"` first, then `job start` with no `--image` (preserves the command) — mirrors how the api/worker steps already update the image.

**Bug 2 — frontend pnpm setup.**
`frontend/package.json:62` *does* have `packageManager: pnpm@9.15.4`, but the `pnpm/action-setup@v4` step runs at repo root and reads the **root** `package.json` (no `packageManager`), so it errors "No pnpm version is specified." The `cache-dependency-path` was set on `setup-node`, not on the pnpm action.
Fix: give the pnpm action `with: version: 9` (or `package_json_file: frontend/package.json`).

**Production is intact.** The migration gate did its job — it aborted *before* the API/worker rollout, so they're still on `v4`, and the frontend job died at setup so the SWA wasn't touched. Nothing rolled.

Per the working agreements (defects → GitHub issue, no silent fixes), I'd file two issues and fix both in one branch (`fix/deploy-migrate-command-and-pnpm-setup`, two commits, `Fixes #N` each), verify locally, and open a PR. Want me to go ahead?

cd /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ
ISSUE1=$(gh issue create --title "Deploy: migrate job runs uvicorn instead of \`alembic upgrade head\` (job start --image drops the configured command)" \
  --label bug --label ci --label priority/P1 --milestone "Week 7 — Deployment, hardening & docs" \
  --body "## Summary
The \`Deploy\` workflow's migration step never runs Alembic — it boots the image's default \`uvicorn\` CMD instead, which fails on \`RuntimeError: Auth not configured\` and aborts the deploy (fail-closed, so the API/worker rollout is correctly skipped).

First surfaced on the first real end-to-end run of the workflow: [run 28334254504](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/actions/runs/28334254504).

## Root cause
The migrate Container Apps Job is defined correctly in Terraform — \`deploy/terraform/containerapps.tf:187\`:
\`\`\`hcl
command = [\"sh\", \"-c\", \"cd backend && alembic upgrade head\"]
\`\`\`
But the workflow starts it with:
\`\`\`
az containerapp job start --name <job> --image \"\$IMAGE\" ...
\`\`\`
Passing \`--image\` to \`job start\` replaces the container template **without** carrying the configured \`command\`, so the execution falls back to the image's Docker \`CMD\` (\`uvicorn ...\`). Migration log confirms uvicorn lifespan startup → \`init_auth()\` → \`Auth not configured\`.

## Fix
Update the job's image first, then start it without an image override so the template command is preserved:
\`\`\`
az containerapp job update --name <job> -g <rg> --image \"\$IMAGE\"
az containerapp job start  --name <job> -g <rg>
\`\`\`
(mirrors how the api/worker steps already \`az containerapp update --image\`).

## Notes
- Production was unaffected — the migration gate aborted before any rollout; api/worker remain on \`v4\`." \
  2>&1)
echo "ISSUE1=$ISSUE1"

ISSUE2=$(gh issue create --title "Deploy: frontend pnpm/action-setup fails — reads root package.json, no pnpm version" \
  --label bug --label ci --label priority/P1 --milestone "Week 7 — Deployment, hardening & docs" \
  --body "## Summary
The \`Deploy\` workflow's frontend job fails at \`pnpm/action-setup@v4\` with \`Error: No pnpm version is specified.\`

First surfaced on [run 28334254504](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/actions/runs/28334254504).

## Root cause
\`packageManager: pnpm@9.15.4\` exists in \`frontend/package.json:62\`, but \`pnpm/action-setup@v4\` runs at the repo root and reads the **root** \`package.json\`, which has no \`packageManager\` field. The \`cache-dependency-path: frontend/pnpm-lock.yaml\` is set on the \`setup-node\` step, not on the pnpm action, so it doesn't help.

## Fix
Pin the version on the pnpm action explicitly:
\`\`\`yaml
- uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
  with:
    version: 9
\`\`\`
(or \`package_json_file: frontend/package.json\`)." \
  2>&1)
echo "ISSUE2=$ISSUE2"

name: Deploy

# Manual-only for now (workflow_dispatch): apply-ready scaffolding that never
# fires on its own until the Azure resources exist + secrets/vars are set. Once
# the infra is provisioned (see deploy/README.md), flip on the push trigger:
#   push:
#     branches: [main]
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      image_tag:
        description: "Image tag to build + deploy"
        default: "latest"

concurrency:
  group: deploy-production
  cancel-in-progress: false

# OIDC federated login to Azure (no stored client secret). The federated cred +
# the listed vars/secrets must be configured first — see deploy/README.md.
# packages:write lets the GITHUB_TOKEN push the backend image to GHCR (ADR 0023).
permissions:
  id-token: write
  contents: read
  packages: write

env:
  # GHCR registry (ADR 0023) — public package, lowercase owner. ACA pulls it
  # anonymously, so no registry credential is stored on the apps/job.
  IMAGE: ghcr.io/theurgicduke771/dataq-backend:${{ inputs.image_tag }}

jobs:
  backend:
    name: Backend — build, migrate, deploy (API + worker)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: production
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Azure login (OIDC)
        uses: azure/login@v2
        with:
          client-id: ${{ secrets.AZURE_CLIENT_ID }}
          tenant-id: ${{ secrets.AZURE_TENANT_ID }}
          subscription-id: ${{ secrets.AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID }}

      # Push to GHCR (ADR 0023). The GITHUB_TOKEN has packages:write on this repo's
      # registry; no Azure/ACR credential is involved in the image push.
      - name: Log in to GHCR
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ghcr.io
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Build + push backend image to GHCR
        run: |
          docker build -f backend/Dockerfile -t "$IMAGE" .
          docker push "$IMAGE"

      # Migrate BEFORE rolling the app: migrations are additive/backward-compatible
      # (CLAUDE.md), so the running old code tolerates the new schema. Runs the
      # one-off migrate Container Apps Job (provisioned per deploy/README.md).
      # `az containerapp job start` only *starts* the execution and returns, so we
      # capture the execution name and poll it to a terminal state — the rollout
      # below must not begin until the migration has actually SUCCEEDED, and a
      # failed migration must abort the deploy (fail-closed) rather than roll the
      # apps onto an un-migrated schema.
      - name: Run DB migrations (alembic upgrade head) and wait for success
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          exec_name=$(az containerapp job start \
            --name "${{ vars.MIGRATE_JOB_NAME }}" \
            --resource-group "${{ vars.AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP }}" \
            --image "$IMAGE" \
            --query name -o tsv)
          echo "Started migration execution: $exec_name"
          deadline=$(( SECONDS + 900 ))  # 15-minute cap
          while (( SECONDS < deadline )); do
            status=$(az containerapp job execution list \
              --name "${{ vars.MIGRATE_JOB_NAME }}" \
              --resource-group "${{ vars.AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP }}" \
              --query "[?name=='$exec_name'].properties.status | [0]" -o tsv)
            echo "migration status: ${status:-<pending>}"
            case "$status" in
              Succeeded) echo "Migration succeeded."; exit 0 ;;
              Failed|Degraded|Stopped) echo "::error::Migration $status — aborting before app rollout."; exit 1 ;;
            esac
            sleep 10
          done
          echo "::error::Timed out waiting for migration to complete."; exit 1

      - name: Deploy API
        run: |
          az containerapp update \
            --name "${{ vars.API_APP_NAME }}" \
            --resource-group "${{ vars.AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP }}" \
            --image "$IMAGE"

      - name: Deploy worker
        run: |
          az containerapp update \
            --name "${{ vars.WORKER_APP_NAME }}" \
            --resource-group "${{ vars.AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP }}" \
            --image "$IMAGE"

  frontend:
    name: Frontend — build + deploy to Static Web App
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: production
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "22"
          cache: pnpm
          cache-dependency-path: frontend/pnpm-lock.yaml

      - name: Build
        working-directory: frontend
        # VITE_* are inlined at build time. Non-secret azure client/tenant ids
        # come from repo vars; never the SPA secret (there is none — MSAL is public).
        env:
          VITE_AZURE_TENANT_ID: ${{ vars.VITE_AZURE_TENANT_ID }}
          VITE_AZURE_SPA_CLIENT_ID: ${{ vars.VITE_AZURE_SPA_CLIENT_ID }}
          VITE_AZURE_API_CLIENT_ID: ${{ vars.VITE_AZURE_API_CLIENT_ID }}
          VITE_AZURE_API_SCOPE: ${{ vars.VITE_AZURE_API_SCOPE }}
          VITE_AUTH_DEV_BYPASS: "false"
        run: |
          pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
          pnpm build

      - name: Deploy to Azure Static Web Apps
        uses: Azure/static-web-apps-deploy@v1
        with:
          azure_static_web_apps_api_token: ${{ secrets.AZURE_STATIC_WEB_APPS_API_TOKEN }}
          action: upload
          app_location: frontend/dist
          skip_app_build: true
